signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5891. COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading hawkish bias) vs RBNZ hike probability priced for today's meeting. With NZD real rates at -0.83% (most accommodative in G8), a hawkish delivery or signal from RBNZ triggers significant short-squeeze risk as crowded spec shorts unwind. NZD/USD vs NZX 50 correlation at +0.362 (z=+1.83σ) confirms NZD is decoupling from local equities to follow rate pricing.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Options Skew + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.54. 282bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry advantage sustains the bid, but 25d RR at -1.22 (moderate put skew) signals institutional hedging against JPY strength. JPY COT LF net short -81,624 remains crowded. 160.00 remains the critical watch level for BoJ verbal warnings or physical intervention; carry dominates the regime while VIX remains at 16.8 (normal volatility).

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7136. IV at 16.6% vs HV30 at 8.3% generates a +8.3% VRP, the highest in the G8, signaling extreme hedging demand. Retail sentiment is 68% short, diverging from COT LF net long +57,180 (74.5% long). This institutional vs retail divergence at an extreme suggests retail is fading the trend; capitulation risk rises if price holds above 0.7150. AUD real rate at -0.22% remains accommodative despite RBA hiking stance.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + Macro Headwinds
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1625. IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.2% (VRP +2.9%) indicates the options market is pricing in higher forward risk than realized. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.00% (175bp spread) structurally favors USD carry. ECB's de Guindos citing supply shocks and lower growth expectations reinforces the bearish macro backdrop. Watch 1.1600 for a clean break to confirm downside extension.
